rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 15, 2013, Line 1, OTHER party NAIM JAMALI's motion to Quash Service. Denied. The Court has jurisdiction over Naim Jamali under Code of Civ. Pro. section 996.420. Defendants' request for attorney's fees and costs is denied. rulings.Case Title: MARIANNE CUNNINGHAM et al VS. GOLDEN GATE HEALTH CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Specially Set Mtn For Terminating Sanctions Or Compelling Deposition And Specially Set Ime And Osc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 15, 2013, Line 1. Defendant KINDRED NURSING CENTER WEST LLC dba GOLDEN GATE HEALTHCARE CENTER'S Motion to Specially Set Motion for Terminating Sanctions or Compelling Deposition and Specially Set IME and OSC Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE HEALTH CENTER'S MOTION IS DENIED. UNDER THE PROVISIONS OF SECTION 1008(a), CCP, THE COURT LACK'S JURISDICTION TO GRANT THE RELIEF NOW REQUESTED. DEFENDANT SHOULD HAVE BROUGHT THIS MOTION WITHIN THE 10 DAYS AFTER IT RECEIVED NOTICE OF THE JANUARY 16, 2013 ORDER. PLAINTIFF SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT A COPY TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND PRESENT THE ORDER FOR SIGNATURE BY THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON MAY 15, 2013. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LAUREE PRIETO VS. B&H EDUCATION INC. DBA MARINELLO SCHOOLS OF BEAUTY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Or Stay Or Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 15, 2013, Line 11. Plaintiff LAUREE PRIETO'S Motion to Quash Service of Summons or Stay or Dismiss Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF LAUREE PRIETO'S MOTION TO QUASH THE SUBPOENA DIRECTED TO URBS ORGANIC SKINCARE IS GRANTED. THE SCOPE OF THE SUBPOENA IS OVERLY BROAD AND NOT LIMITED TO RELEVANT ISSUES IN THE LITIGATION. IN ADDITION, SERIOUS QUESTIONS ARE RAISED AS TO THE APPROPRIATENESS OF DEFENDANT'S UTILIZATION OF AN OUT OF STATE SUBPOENA TO OBTAIN INFORMATION WHICH IT COULD HAVE OBTAINED UTILIZING THE NORMAL DISCOVERY PROCESSES PROVIDED UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE ASSESSED AS IT APPEARS FROM THE FILES THAT PLAINTIFF IN ITS RESPONSES TO OTHER DISCOVERY REQUESTS HAS FAILED TO RESPOND IN A MANNER CONSISTENT WITH THE GOOD FAITH REQUIREMENTS OF CALIFORNIA DISCOVERY STATUTES AND DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO UTILIZE THE PROCESSES TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH THOSE REQUIREMENTS. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF WILL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON MAY 15, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GOLDSTEIN, GELLMAN, MELBOSTAD, HARRIS & MCSPARRAN, VS. DEAN RODATOS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 15, 2013, Line 6. Plaintiff GOLDSTEIN, GELLMAN, MELBOSTAD, HARRIS & MCSPARRAN'S (Amended) Notice of Motion and Motion to Compel Production of Documents Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. A COPY OF DEFENDANTS' OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (SET ONE) WAS NOT DELIVERED TO DEPARTMENT 302 AS REQUIRED; HOWEVER, FROM THE CONTENTS OF PLAINTIFF'S REPLY MEMORANDUM THE COURT CAN SURMISE THE THRUST OF DEFENDANTS' OBJECTION AND DO NOT FIND THAT THEY ARE SUFFICIENT TO EXCUSE DEFENDANTS FAILURE TO SUPPLY PLAINTIFF WITH THE DOCUMENTS PROMISED IN THEIR RESPONSE TO THE REQUEST. DEFENDANT WILL DELIVER THE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS WITHIN 10 DAYS OF THIS ORDER. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WENDY OAKES VS. CELTIC MOVING & STORAGE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Response To And Compliance With Deft'S Request For Production, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 15, 2013, Line 7. Defendant CELTIC MOVING & STORAGE, INC.'S Motion to Compel Further Response to and Compliance with Defendant's Request for Production, Set One Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: IT APPEARS FROM BOTH THE MOVING AND OPPOSITION PAPERS THAT THE DOCUMENTS REQUESTED IN REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION (SET ONE) REQUEST NO 3 HAVE BEEN PRODUCED IN REDACTED FORM AND DEFENDANT CELTIC MOVING & STORAGE, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL SEEKS UNREDACTED RESPONSES. NEITHER PARTY HAS SUPPLIED THE COURT WITH COPIES OF THE REDACTED DOCUMENTS WHICH MAKES IT DIFFICULT TO DETERMINE IF THE REDACTED DOCUMENTS ARE SUFFICIENT COMPLIANCE WITH THE REQUEST. TO ASSIST THE PARTIES IN RESOLVING THIS MATTER THE COURT IS PREPARED TO ISSUE AN ORDER WHICH, SUBJECT TO AN APPROPRIATE PROTECTIVE ORDER, COMPELS PLAINTIFF TO PROVIDE TO DEFENDANT WITHIN TEN DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER COPIES OF BANK STATEMENTS FOR THE TWO ACCOUNTS IDENTIFIED IN RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO 19 FOR THE RELEVANT TIME PERIOD IN QUESTION REDACTING ALL INFORMATION REFLECTING SPECIFIC TRANSACTIONS, BUT SHOWING ALL BALANCES IN THE ACCOUNTS DURING THE RELEVANT TIME PERIOD. I DO NOT BELIEVE THAT DEFENDANT HAS MADE A SUFFICIENT SHOWING OF RELEVANCE OF THE SPECIFIC DAILY ACTIVITY IN THE ACCOUNTS TO OVERCOME THE PUBLIC POLICY OF PRIVACY CONTAINED IN CALIFORNIA LAW. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE ASSESSED. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF WILL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT IS TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON MAY 15, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THE BAUGHMAN COMPANY INC, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. JOACHIM BERGMANN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 15, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANTs JOACHIM BERGMANN, ALEX ZWERDLING & BERGMANN ZWERDLING DIRECT's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendant's demurrer to cause of action 1 for misappropriation of trade secrets is overruled. Taking the facts in the Complaint as true, Plaintiff has pled that the alleged Customer List is a trade secret because it has "independent economic value that is derived from not being known to the public or to persons who could obtain economic value from its disclosure or use. Plaintiff took steps that were reasonable under the circumstances to maintain the secrecy of the list and information¿." (Complaint 32); (See Cal. Civ. Code § 3426.1(d).) Defendant's demurrer to cause of action 2 for breach of contract is overruled. Taking the facts as true, the alleged covenant to the employment contract was written for the narrow purpose of protecting the confidential client information used by Plaintiff in his business. (See Kolani v. Gluska (1998) Cal.App.4th 402, 408) (recognizing covenants with "narrower contractual restraints on a departing employee, which prohibit him/her from using confidential information taken from the former employer, have been held to be lawful." ) Defendant's demurrer to cause of action 3 for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing is sustained with leave to amend. While Plaintiff has sufficiently pled the Defendant Zwerdling's written employment contract in haec verba, Plaintiff has failed to plead sufficient facts to determine if Defendant Bergmann's employment contract was written or oral. Defendants are given 10 days leave to amend to allege facts in good faith as to the type of contract that was entered by Plaintiff and Defendant Bergmann, and to plead the contract in haec verba and/or "according to its legal intendment and effect." (See Construction Protective Services, Inc. v. TIG Specialty Ins. Co. (2002) 29 Cal.App.4th 189, 198-199.) Defendant's demurrer to causes of action 4, 5, 6, and 9 are sustained without leave to amend. The allegations are based on the same nucleus of facts as the misappropriation of trade secrets claim for relief. (See K.C. Multimedia, Inc. v. Bank of American Technology & Operations, Inc. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 939, 958.) Defendant's demurrer to cause of action 7 for violation of California Penal Code S 502 is overruled. Taking the facts as true, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts that Defendants accessed Plaintiff's computer system to obtain confidential information, and that the alleged not performed within the scope of their employment. See CPC § 502. Defendant's demurrer to cause of action 8 for conversion is sustained with leave to amend. Defendants are given 10 days leave to amend to allege facts in good faith that the destroyed emails have independent value from the information included them. See Mattel, Inc. v. MGA Entm't, Inc., 782 F. Supp. 2d 911, 997 (C.D. Cal. 2011) (applying California law). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
